Abstract in English
English
### *Archaeology of Medieval Jewish Cemeteries in France* According to textual sources, there are some 140 Jewish cemeteries dating from the Middle Ages in present-day France. Few of these have been precisely located, and even fewer have been studied by archaeologists, as only five "houses of the living" have been scientifically excavated. title: Archaeological Interventions in Medieval Jewish Cemeteries in Western Europe
highlights:
Keywords: funerary practices, burial methods, cemeteries, Middle Ages, Jewish archaeology, Jewish communities
## Abstract This article presents the main Jewish cemeteries of the Medieval period that have been the object of archaeological intervention in several countries of Western Europe (France, Spain, England, Italy, Switzerland).
